title: HBO Finds Perfect ‘The Last of Us’ Replacement in Ambitious New Fantasy Series

blocks.content: HBO has released plenty of popular shows in the last few years, but few have risen to the same levels of popularity as The Last of Us, which is based on the pair of award-winning video games developed by Naughty Dog. The first season of The Last of Us aired back in 2023, and the show went on hiatus for two years before returning for Season 2 in 2023. Production on The Last of Us Season 3 recently came to a scheduled halt due to the World Cup, but all signs point to the show still having no trouble returning in 2027. The show was written and created for TV by Craig Mazin and Neil Druckmann, and after Druckmann departed the series to work on Naughty Dog's next game, Intergalactic: The Heretic Prophet, Mazin is now tasked with bringing the show to the finish line for its third and final season.

After also working on another critically acclaimed HBO show, Chernobyl, Mazin has reached the level that fans are always curious to know what he’s working on next. News broke earlier this year that Mazin is actively developing a new series for HBO based on the Game of the Year-winning video game, Baldur’s Gate 3, which won in 2023 over other popular titles like Spider-Man 2. It’s unclear at this time when the show will be released, but HBO Max boss Casey Bloys recently shed some light about when Mazin plans to begin shooting the new fantasy series, confirming that "he's going to focus on The Last of Us, and then come back to [Baldur’s Gate] when he's got more time." Nothing is known about the Baldur's Gate show at this time, and more information will likely be made available later this year or early in 2027.
Your answers point to the iconic universe your values, your instincts, and your particular way of seeing the world were built for. This is where you would find your people — and your purpose.
You believe in the cause — in the idea that freedom is worth fighting for even when the odds are impossible and the empire is vast.
You understand, in the deepest part of yourself, that the journey matters as much as the destination — and that the world's beauty is worth protecting even at great cost.
You believe that love, loyalty, and doing what's right are not naive sentiments — they are the most powerful forces in any world, magical or otherwise.
You see the world clearly — its power structures, its hypocrisies, its brutal arithmetic — and you are not paralysed by that clarity. You use it.
You believe the future is worth building — that curiosity, cooperation, and the expansion of understanding are not just ideals but the most practical path forward for any civilisation.
Why Is ‘The Last of Us’ Ending With Season 3?

The first season of The Last of Us adapted the entire first game in the franchise, and Season 2 adapted only the Ellie storyline from The Last of Us Part II. Season 3 will tell Abby’s side of the story, with Kaitlyn Dever stepping into a much larger role for the next batch of episodes. Once Abby’s story is finished, HBO’s The Last of Us series will have told the entire story from The Last of Us Part II, meaning that it would need to go off-script to continue into Season 4 and beyond. With Druckmann departing the show to go back to working on video games, it’s highly unlikely Mazin has any desire to take these characters outside the confines of the source material.
Check out the first two seasons of The Last of Us on HBO Max and stay tuned to Collider for more updates and coverage of Season 3.
